# truenas_nfs_share (Resource)

Manages a TrueNAS NFS share.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "truenas_pool_dataset" "exports" {
  name = "tank/exports"
}

resource "truenas_nfs_share" "exports" {
  path     = truenas_pool_dataset.exports.mountpoint
  comment  = "NFS exports"
  networks = ["192.168.1.0/24"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `path` (String) The filesystem path to share (e.g. /mnt/tank/data).

### Optional

- `comment` (String) Description of the share.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the share is enabled. Defaults to true.
- `hosts` (List of String) List of allowed hostnames or IP addresses.
- `mapall_group` (String) Map all requests to this group.
- `mapall_user` (String) Map all requests to this user.
- `maproot_group` (String) Map root requests to this group.
- `maproot_user` (String) Map root requests to this user.
- `networks` (List of String) List of allowed networks in CIDR notation (e.g. 192.168.1.0/24).

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The unique identifier of the NFS share.
- `locked` (Boolean) Whether the share is locked.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import truenas_nfs_share.example 1
```
